heading: McKellars Park vs McLeods Shoot — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McKellars Park, New South Wales and McLeods Shoot, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McKellars Park has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to McLeods Shoot (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McKellars Park or McLeods Shoot safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McKellars Park has a flood risk score of 52/100, while McLeods Shoot scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McKellars Park scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. McLeods Shoot scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McKellars Park or McLeods Shoot?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McKellars Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
